facts.text: Freeport became an incorporated village on October 18, 1892, marking the start of its formal local government.
WGBB, founded in 1924, was Long Island’s first 24‑hour radio station, broadcasting nonstop from Freeport.
From 1931 until the early 1980s, Freeport hosted the Freeport Speedway, a venue that drew auto racing fans for decades.
Between 1970 and 1973, Freeport High School’s math team earned regional acclaim, competing in the International Mathematical Olympiad.
A short‑lived trolley called the “Fishermen’s Delight” ran along Grove Street (now Guy Lombardo Avenue) from 1913, linking the waterfront to Sunrise Highway.
The Freeport History Encyclopedia, housed in the local library, offers authoritative biographies and stories that chronicle the village’s past.
